findings: Independent verification agrees with the plate: Cyanocitta cristata is a corvid with a prominent movable crest, blue upperparts, pale gray-white underparts, black facial markings and necklace, a strong straight dark bill, dark eyes, black-barred wings and tail, white wingbars, and white tail corners.
The stated length (25–30 cm), wingspan (34–43 cm), and weight (70–100 g) exactly match Cornell’s published ranges; Audubon independently corroborates the same metric ranges.

Visible anatomy is coherent for a Blue Jay: one main head and beak, two legs and grasping feet, a long rounded barred tail, and the pair of wings is represented by the folded wing plus the supplemental spread-wing study. The separate head and wing studies are clearly plate insets, not extra anatomy.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.
No material species-identity or text errors found.

verification_sources.title: Blue Jay Identification — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
